title: rosa gallica subsp. austriaca
intro: Rosa gallica subsp. austriaca, also known as Austrian briar or Austrian rose, is a subspecies of the Gallic rose and a member of the rose family (Rosaceae). This perennial shrub has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa gallica subsp. austriaca include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and antiviral activities. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
